assertion_results.text: Each task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 task files (task-1 through task-6) contain every required section. Task 1 has Repository (trustify-backend), Target Branch (main), Description, Files to Create, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 2 has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Task 3 has all including Files to Create and Files to Modify. Task 4 has all with Files to Modify. Task 5 has all with Files to Modify. Task 6 has all including Files to Create and Files to Modify.

assertion_results.text: Task count is between 3 and 10 inclusive
assertion_results.evidence: 6 task files exist: task-1-severity-summary-model.md, task-2-severity-aggregation-service.md, task-3-advisory-summary-endpoint.md, task-4-threshold-query-param.md, task-5-cache-invalidation.md, task-6-integration-tests.md. 6 is within [3, 10].
All 6 tasks have '## Repository\ntrustify-backend' as the first section. Verified in task-1 (line 1-2), task-2 (line 1-2), task-3 (line 1-2), task-4 (line 1-2), task-5 (line 1-2), task-6 (line 1-2).

assertion_results.text: Task dependencies form a DAG with no circular dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: Dependency graph: Task 1 has no dependencies. Task 2 depends on Task 1 (line 45). Task 3 depends on Task 2 (line 52). Task 4 depends on Task 3 (line 42). Task 5 depends on Task 3 (line 39). Task 6 depends on Task 4 (line 58). This forms a DAG: 1->2->3->4->6 and 3->5. No cycles exist.

assertion_results.text: Every task's Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths plausible within the repo-backend.md structure
assertion_results.evidence: All file paths are plausible within the repo-backend.md structure. Files to Modify reference existing paths: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (task 1), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (tasks 2, 4), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (task 3), modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs (task 5), tests/Cargo.toml (task 6). Files to Create follow established patterns: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/advisory_severity_summary.rs (task 1, follows model/ pattern), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/advisory_summary.rs (task 3, follows endpoints/ pattern), tests/api/advisory_summary.rs (task 6, follows tests/api/*.rs pattern).

assertion_results.text: Impact map lists concrete, specific changes (not vague or abstract descriptions)
assertion_results.evidence: Impact map in impact-map.md lists 8 concrete changes: 'Add AdvisorySeveritySummary model struct to represent aggregated severity counts', 'Add service method to SbomService that queries advisory-SBOM relationships and aggregates severity counts with deduplication', 'Add GET /api/v2/sbom/{id}/advisory-summary endpoint handler with 5-minute cache configuration', 'Add optional ?threshold query parameter to filter severity counts above a given level', 'Register the new endpoint route in SBOM endpoints module', 'Add cache invalidation hook in advisory ingestion pipeline', 'Add integration tests for the new endpoint covering success, 404, caching, and threshold filtering', 'Update API documentation to include the new endpoint'. All are specific with named endpoints, parameters, and implementation details.

assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks contain '## Repository\ntrustify-backend' with exactly one repository name. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.evidence: All 6 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified across all task files.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a real computed SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters, not a placeholder (<hex-digest>, <hex>), abbreviated value, or example string. Task 4: sha256:0d98db153c07c0cd735d287da7987ec241cb0f4141408c036720b63ad0d7e7a8 (64 chars). Task 5: sha256:aa0eebb03fff47d15d7c8eadcead36802aefb42daba39d12e5dce338c2bab7c9 (64 chars). Task 6: sha256:cfdb87bb48460ae9640dd170036939e3e632db313c3fd8266e3f83c9006e9ef6 (64 chars). All are exactly 64 lowercase hex characters, no placeholders or abbreviations.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No conventions are referenced in any of the 6 task files. None of the tasks contain 'Per CONVENTIONS.md', 'Not applicable', 'N/A', 'Excluded', or any convention annotations. Since the eval uses a synthetic repo-backend.md fixture with no actual CONVENTIONS.md content available, the absence of convention enrichment is expected and acceptable. No inapplicable conventions are listed.
